title: Patrizia Secures EUR 351 Million Refinancing for Major Underground Energy Storage Asset in Germany
content:
PATRIZIA, an investment manager in global real assets, has secured EUR 351 million in refinancing to support the long-term development of the Etzel storage facility, a major underground energy storage asset in northern Germany, which holds essential energy reserves for Germany and north-west Europe. The financing, which extends loan maturities by five years and reduces pricing, is part of a wider EUR 540 million debt package arranged by Commerzbank AG.

The facility currently consists of 75 operational caverns that store vital liquid and gas reserves and has the potential to expand to up to 99 caverns in the future. For more than 50 years, the asset has served as a critical component of Germanyâ€™s oil and gas energy infrastructure. As the energy transition advances, its role will increasingly shift toward large-scale hydrogen storage, supporting the decarbonisation of industry and the wider energy system.

Heiko SÃ¼ÃŸ, Managing Director at PATRIZIA, said: â€œThis refinancing allows us to progress the next phase of development for one of Europeâ€™s most important energy storage assets. The backing of our lenders reinforces the long-term future of the facility as a hydrogen-ready platform central to Europeâ€™s industrial decarbonisation efforts.â€
author: Analysts Account
